ClinicalDocument.title: Mental Health and Substance Misuse Specialist Services Care Programme Approach Review Summary

code.displayName: Care Process Date Commenced or First Review
Care Process Date Concluded or Most Recent Review

value.displayName: Individual Issue (Description of Issue for Individual)
Individual Issue (Description of Issue for Individual)

code.displayName: Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 1 - aggressive/distruptive behaviour
Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 2 - non-accidental self injury
Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 3 - alcohol/drug problem
Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 4 - cognitive problems
Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 5 - physical illness/disability.
Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 6 - hallucinations/delusions.
Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 7 - depressed mood.

Health of the Nation Outcome Scale item 12 - occupation/activities.
Health of the Nation Outcome Scale (generic version)

section.title: Aliases or Other Names
section.text: Tony often uses a nickname 'Maca'.

section.title: Communication and Comprehension
section.text: Tony needs his hearing aid to hear clearly - a visit from an Audiologist has provided Tony with a new rechargeable hearing aid - although he does still need support to ensure he keeps it recharged and cleaned. Native Language Irish.

section.title: Marital or Partnership Status
section.text: Formally Separated.

section.title: Housing
section.text: Squatting.Living in a Communal Setting: This is a squat that Tony shares with 5 other people. The Squat has recently been served with an eviction order and Tony will not be able to return to this address

section.title: Property Access Information
section.text: The Squat is not accessible and its residents use barricades to prevent anyone getting in to evict them.

section.title: Ethnicity, Religion, Philosophy & Culture
section.text:
Tony has expressed a wish to attend the Church of the Virgin Mary, Uptown some Sunday mornings. Ethnicity: White Irish.
Religion Philosophy: Roman Catholic Church.

section.title: Education, Training, Employment and Financial Status
section.text: Tony has recently been attending a local IT beginner’s course having expressed an interest in learning about computers.

section.title: Financial Status
section.text: Before admission to Hospital Tony was in receipt of Jobseekers Allowance, he has since been in receipt of Incapacity Benefit 02/04/2004 – ongoing.

section.title: Referral, First Contact, Triage and Entry
Referral / First Contact
section.text: Referral Date: 01/01/2004

section.title: Reason for Referral / First Contact
section.text: Tony was expressing some grandiose ideas about who he is that lead staff at the hostel to become concerned for him. He can be aggressive and personally invasive, however he had not been observed being physically violent.

section.title: Triage Decision
section.text: Date Recorded: 01/01/2004. URGENT. The referral indicates that it is important that Tony is seen urgently.

section.title: Specialist Service Assessment
section.text: Date of Entry: 01/01/2004

section.title: Presenting Problems
section.text: Tony was expressing grandiose ideas and was aggressive and invasive.

section.title: Outcome of Specialist Service Assessment
section.text: Tony's symptoms indicate hypomania.

section.title: Entry to MH&SM Specialist Services
section.text: Entry Date: 01/01/2004

section.title: Specialist Services Entry Decision Summary
section.text: The decision was taken to admit Tony for assessment and treatment

section.title: Further Details or Concerns Relating to Current Contact Status
section.text: This is Tony's new address for the period of his detention.

section.title: Care Teams or Organisations Involved in Care Delivery
Main Care Team
section.text: Date when the first entry for this team was recorded = 04/05/2004.

section.title: Involvement in Patient Care or Support
section.text: Adult Mental Health Inpatient Unit

section.title: Identifiers Used In Other Organisations
section.text: National Insurance No = AA 01 01 01A

section.title: Additional Care Team
section.text: Date when the first entry for this team was recorded = 03/03/2004

section.title: Involvement in Patient Care or Support
section.text: Uptown Mental Health Trust Occupational Therapy Team - assisting Tony in some of his domestic and socialising activities.

section.title: Persons with a Connection to the Care of Patient

tr.td:
Tony's Mother - Margaret undertook the role of Nearest Relative when he was sectioned.
Carer ID: 98765432

MHA Nearest Relative - Tony's Mother.
Last assessment conducted on 09/02/2004.

Andrew is Tony's Older Brother and provides all the support for his financial and legal affairs and will advocate on his behalf for all activities where advocacy may be required.
Carer ID: 23456789
Person Holding a Power of Attornay.

Shelter Hostel
Jones Street
Leeds
LS1 1AA
Andrew Smith is the Hostel Manager for Shelter. He has a good relationship with Tony and is able to get through to him sometimes when he is otherwise difficult to engage with.
Care Coordinator/Lead Professional/Primary Key Worker at Uptown Social Services

section.title: Agreement of HSCPs / Carers to Review and Conclusions
section.text: All the participants agreed to the conclusions of the review and its outcomes

section.title: Summary of Review Outcomes
section.text:
The following aspects of care are detailed in the care components section of this review:
Medication management
Accommodation difficulties
Lack of structure to day / meaningful occupation
Financial difficulties
Treatment and monitoring of bipolar disorder
Management of drug and alcohol misuse

section.title: Patient / Service User Involvement in Review
section.text: Date Summary Provided: 13/06/2004

section.title: Patients Involvement In The Review
section.text: Tony declined to attend the review, however I was able to see him the same day in the evening.

section.title: Patient / Service User Agreement to Review
section.text: Tony accepted the new care plan and said he was particularly looking forward to the social worker helping him to find somewhere better to live for when he is discharged.

section.title: Location of Signed Paper Copy of Agreement
section.text: Ward Office in patient paper record.

section.title: Means used to provide a Summary Copy of the review for the Patient / Service User
section.text: Paper copy.

section.title: Diagnosis
section.text:
Hypomania diagnosedon 12/2/2004.
ICD-10: F30

section.title: Registered Disability / Other Condition(s)
section.text: Tony has a hearing problem for which he has seen an Audiologist and has been fitted with appropriate new hearing aids.

section.title: Care Treatment Support Required
section.text: Suggest Tony attend a hearing clinic to determine the exact cause of his hearing problem as well as further care required.

section.title: Problem Substances Information
section.text:
Primary Substance: Alcohol.
Age of first use = 12.
Taken Orally

tr.td: MHA Department HEBBURN MENTAL HEALTH TRUST CAMPBELL PARK ROAD TYNE & WEAR NE31 2SP on 01/01/2004 at 11:30.
End of the period where papers authorised for enacting a section may be served:

section.title: Reason for MHA Assessment
section.text: Following some bizarre comments and expressions by Tony the drop-in centre manager called the local Social Services Department who instigated this MHA Assessment.

section.title: Outcome of Assessment
section.text: It was agreed that Tony requires further assessment and so an application for a Section was made.

section.title: Legal Status and MHA Details
section.text: Summary: Detained in hospital under Section 3 of the Mental Health Act 1983 (England and Wales) as of 01/01/2004 : 10:30.

section.title: MHA Section Conditions
section.text: Tony is to be detained at Uptown Inpatient Mental Health Unit.

list.item: Lack of structure to day / meaningful occupation
Treatment and monitoring of bipolar disorder
Management of drug and alcohol misuse

section.title: Care Plan Component: Medication Management
section.text:
Tony needs to take medication regularly to treat his mental illness.
Aims and Objectives:
Tony to take medication regularly and safely.
Tony to be prescribed appropriate amounts of medication that successfully treat his illness and keep side effects to a minimum.
Interventions & Actions:
Education regarding need to take medication regularly. /Information about medication used and how to take and store it safely.
Regular monitoring of efficacy and side effects.
Regular blood tests for Clozapine prescriptions - blood results sent to Clozapine monitoring service.
Blood tests for lithium levels.
Prescriptions for clozapine to be issued by CMHT medical staff following clearance from clozapine monitoring service.
All other prescriptions to be issued by GP.
Liaison with community pharmacist to ensure their understanding of clozapine prescription process and regular supply of medication.
Regular monitoring of weight, blood pressure and blood sugar levels.
Education regarding need to maintain a healthy lifestyle, including exercise regime. GP to be advised of responsibility.
Frequency of Contact:
Weekly visits from CMHN.
6 weekly outpatient appointments with Consultant Psychiatrist.
Attendance at Clozapine clinic for blood tests - variable frequency depending on results.
Fortnightly attendances at GP surgery to see practice nurse.
Weekly prescriptions from GP to be collected by Tony from local pharmacy.
Care Plan component Start Date: 03/03/2004
Date for this Care Plan component Review:
01/09/2004
Care Plan Component Review Status: Review Not Reached
GP will have responsibility for this component.

section.title: Care Plan Component: Accommodation
section.text:
Tony was living in a Squat before he was admitted; there is a need to support him to find more appropriate accommodation.
Aims and Objectives:
To find suitable accommodation - possibly with one of the local housing associations.
Interventions & Actions:
Ask Social Worker - Joan Harper to support Tony in this.
Care Plan component Start Date: 03/03/2004
Date for this Care Plan component Review:
01/09/2004
Care Plan Component Review Status: Review Not Reached

tr.td: Tony stays the day with Andrew his older brother

section.title: Contingency Plan
section.text: If Tony deteriorates the Uptown CMHT should be contacted to see if further care is needed. This will have to be treated as a new care spell and an MHA Assessment will need doing - if not done already.

section.title: Relapse Indicators
section.text: Increased use of Alcohol. Hypomania symptoms. Irritability.

section.title: Crisis Plan
section.text: In the event that Tony absconds, contact the Police.

section.title: Risk Assessment Categories and Scale Scores

tr.td: Tony has not shown any indications that he is suicidal or that his mood is severely depressed.
He has not shown any indications of intentional self-harm.
Tony can be clumsy and his ability to move quickly has been affected by the consumption of alcohol over time.
During particularly heavy drinking bouts, Tony may go for days without attention to personal hygiene, appearance or proper dietary intake.
Tony is at his most vulnerable when under the influence of alcohol, however his aggressive behaviour does tend to ward-off anyone attempting to approach him.
Tony becomes verbally abusive when he doesn't get his way. Although he has not actually been physically violent he can be extremely intimidating.
When under the influence of alcohol, Tony can be extremely aggressive - this aggression has caused him to be arrested several times for breaches of the peace and using threatening behaviour.
The level of abuse is stricly limited to the effects of alcohol upon him - he does seem to manipulate or hurt others intentionally.
There are no persons dependent upon Tony at this time, however it would be unlikely that he could take on any such responsibilities.

section.title: HCR20 Violence Risk Assessment
section.text: HCR20 Assessment Date: 01/01/2004

section.title: HCR20 Outcome Score
section.text:
Category:
H1 Previous Violence
Scale: 1
Indications have recently come to our attention that in his younger life Tony was convicted of Breach of the Peace connected to criminal activities - however there has not been any recent evidence of violent behaviours.

tr.td: When he has had a considerable amount of alcohol he can be verbally aggressive.

section.title: Risk Activities History
section.text:
Date: 01/05/2002
Category: Aggression to Adults
Level: High
Tony has a long history of aggressive behaviour which whilst it has not seemed to extend into physical violence. He was arrested for a breach of the peace in May 2002 and was bound over to keep the peace at that time.

tr.td: HoNOS item 10 - activities of daily living

section.title: Additional Information
section.text: No formal mental health history previously This care episode commenced on 01/01/04, with Tony admitted with indications of Hypomania.

section.title: Aspect of Care that a Preference relates to.
Stated Preference Information
section.text: Tony does not want his younger brother Frederick to know anything about his condition.
